LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., VINCENT CHOWS'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST, 2ND, 5TH, 6TH AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CLAIM AGAINST DEFENDANTS. MOREOVER, WHILE DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA HAD NO DUTY TO PLAINTIFFS PURSUANT TO THE AMOUNT OF THE LOAN AND PLAINTIFFS' ABILITY TO PAY THE LOAN, IT HAD A DUTY TO FAIRLY DISCLOSE INFORMATION ABOUT THE NATURE AND TERMS OF THE LOAN. IT ALSO HAD A DUTY TO ENSURE THAT TELLER TRANSACTIONS WERE NOT FRAUDULENTLY CONDUCTED. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF CANNOT SEEK BOTH RESCISSION AND CANCELLATION OF THE INSTRUMENT (BOTH CANCEL THE INSTRUMENT AND KEEP THE FUNDS). MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF MUST OFFER TO RESTORE THE BENEFIT RECEIVED FROM BANK OF AMERICA IN ORDER TO SEEK RESCISSION OF THE LOAN. PLAINTIFF WILL HAVE 15 DAYS (FROM NOTICE OF THIS ORDER) TO FILE AND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason M. Julian, Esq., Severson & Werson, 415-398-3344, for defendant and moving party
Peter B. Brekhus, Esq., Brekhus Law Parteners, 415-461-1001, for plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANTS BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., VINCENT CHOWS'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 1st, 2nd, 5th, 6th and 7th causes of action is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim against defendants. Moreover, while Defendant Bank of America had no duty to Plaintiffs pursuant to the amount of the loan and Plaintiffs' ability to pay the loan, it had a duty to fairly disclose information about the nature and terms of the loan. It also had a duty to ensure that teller transactions were not fraudulently conducted.
Defendants' Demurrer to the 3rd and 4th causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot seek both rescission and Cancellation of the instrument (both cancel the instrument and keep the funds). Moreover, plaintiff must offer to restore the benefit received from Bank of America in order to seek rescission of the loan.
Plaintiff will have 15 days (from notice of this order) to file and amended complaint.
Defendant's attorney to prepare a form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
